question_text: A ball is thrown upwards with a velocity of 20 m/s. At the highest point, what can be said about its energy?

options.label: A
options.text: It has maximum kinetic energy and minimum potential energy

options.label: B
options.text: It has minimum kinetic energy and maximum potential energy

options.label: C
options.text: Both kinetic and potential energy are zero

options.label: D
options.text: It has maximum kinetic and potential energy

explanation: At the highest point, the ball momentarily stops (kinetic energy = 0) before falling back down. All the initial kinetic energy has been converted to gravitational potential energy at this point.
